walkthrough.title: Place the Invader Board
walkthrough.text: Let's set up Spirit Island! First, find the large Invader Board and place it at one side of the play area where everyone can see it. This board tracks the invader pipeline — you will see spaces for the Invader Deck, and three action slots: Ravage, Build, and Explore. The invaders follow this pipeline every round.

walkthrough.title: Build the Fear Pool
walkthrough.text: Find the small Fear marker tokens. Add four Fear markers per player to the Fear Pool area on the Invader Board. So for a two-player game, that is eight markers. For three players, that is twelve. These markers track how much fear you are generating against the invaders.

walkthrough.title: Build the Fear Deck
walkthrough.text: Shuffle all 15 Fear cards. Deal nine cards face down into a pile. Now find the two Terror dividers, labeled Terror Level two and Terror Level three. Insert the Terror three divider three cards from the bottom of the pile, and insert the Terror two divider three cards above that. The remaining six Fear cards go back in the box.

walkthrough.title: Build the Invader Deck
walkthrough.text: Separate the 15 Invader cards by stage — you will see Roman numerals one, two, and three on the backs. Shuffle each stage separately. Remove one random card from each stage without looking at it and put those three cards back in the box. Stack the remaining twelve cards: four Stage one cards on top, four Stage two in the middle, and four Stage three on the bottom.

walkthrough.title: Place the Blight Card
walkthrough.text: Find a random Blight card and place it near the Invader Board with the Healthy side face up. Do not read the back yet — you will only flip it when the island becomes blighted. Place Blight tokens from the supply onto the card: two per player. So for a two-player game, place four Blight tokens on the card.

walkthrough.title: Choose and Arrange Island Boards
walkthrough.text: Randomly pick one Island Board per player from the four available. For a two-player game, pick two boards and arrange them side by side with matching letters on adjacent edges. For three or four players, follow the layout diagrams in the rulebook. Each board represents a section of the island.

walkthrough.title: Populate the Island
walkthrough.text: Each Island Board has icons printed on certain land spaces showing what starts there. Follow these icons to place the starting pieces. You will add Explorers, Towns, Cities, Dahan, and Blight tokens to the lands as shown. This represents the invaders already established on the island and the native Dahan people who live there.

walkthrough.title: Prepare the Power Decks
walkthrough.text: Shuffle the Minor Power deck and the Major Power deck separately. Place them face down near the play area with room for discard piles next to each. These are the powers you will gain throughout the game to grow stronger.

walkthrough.title: Set Up Supply Piles
walkthrough.text: Organize the remaining pieces into accessible supply piles: Explorers, Towns, Cities, Dahan, Energy markers, and Blight tokens. Place them where all players can reach them. You will be adding and removing these pieces from the island throughout the game.

walkthrough.title: Choose Your Spirit
walkthrough.text: Now the exciting part — each player chooses a Spirit. Take that Spirit's panel and its four unique Power cards. For your first game, stick with the low-complexity Spirits. The rulebook recommends specific ones for beginners. Read the front of your Spirit panel to see your special rule and innate powers.

walkthrough.title: Place Starting Presence
walkthrough.text: Flip your Spirit panel to the back. It shows exactly where to place your starting Presence on the island board. Follow the diagram carefully. Then place the remaining Presence tokens on the dashed circles of your Presence tracks on the front of your panel. Leave the leftmost solid circles uncovered — those show your starting energy gain and card play limit.

walkthrough.title: Set Up Presence Tracks
walkthrough.text: The top track shows energy gain per turn, and the bottom track shows how many cards you can play per turn. As you add Presence to the island during the game, you uncover higher values on these tracks. This is how your Spirit grows more powerful over time.

walkthrough.title: First-Game Recommendations
walkthrough.text: Use the Power Progression cards instead of drafting from the power decks. Skip the Adversary and Scenario panels — just play the base difficulty. You may also skip the random Blight card and use the Invader Board's printed blight rules instead. These training wheels help you focus on learning the core gameplay.

walkthrough.title: Start-of-Game Invader Explore
walkthrough.text: Before the first round begins, flip the top Invader card face up. Resolve an Explore action in that land type — add one Explorer to each land of that type that is adjacent to a Town, City, or Ocean. Then slide that card to the Build space on the Invader Board. The invader pipeline is now loaded and you can see your first threat.

walkthrough.title: Ready to Defend the Island
walkthrough.text: Setup is complete. Each player should have their Spirit panel in front of them with Presence tracks visible, four unique Power cards in hand, and Presence tokens on the island. The Invader Board shows the pipeline with one card already in the Build slot. Take a deep breath — the island needs you. Let's learn how to play.

walkthrough.title: The Round Structure
walkthrough.text: Spirit Island is played in rounds, and each round has five phases in order: Spirit Phase, Fast Power Phase, Invader Phase, Slow Power Phase, and Time Passes. Everyone acts together in each phase. There are no individual turns — all players resolve their Spirit Phase at the same time, then all Fast powers resolve, then the invaders act, and so on.

walkthrough.title: Spirit Phase: Growth
walkthrough.text: The Spirit Phase is where you grow and prepare. First, choose one Growth option from the top of your Spirit panel. Growth options let you add Presence to the island, gain Energy, reclaim used Power cards, or gain new Power cards. Each Spirit has different Growth options, and this choice shapes your entire round.

walkthrough.title: Spirit Phase: Gain Energy
walkthrough.text: After choosing Growth, gain Energy equal to the highest uncovered value on your top Presence track. Energy is your currency for playing Power cards. As you place more Presence on the island, you uncover higher values on this track, meaning you gain more Energy each round. Take Energy markers from the supply to track your total.

walkthrough.title: Spirit Phase: Play Power Cards
walkthrough.text: Now choose which Power cards to play this round. You can play up to the number shown on your bottom Presence track. Each card has an Energy cost in the upper left corner — pay that cost from your Energy supply. Place your chosen cards face up in front of you. These powers will resolve later in the round during either the Fast or Slow phase.

walkthrough.title: Fast vs Slow Powers
walkthrough.text: Every Power card has a speed indicator — a bird icon means Fast, a turtle icon means Slow. Fast powers resolve before the invaders act, giving you a chance to prevent damage. Slow powers resolve after the invaders act, letting you clean up or reposition. Timing is everything. A well-placed Fast power can stop a devastating Ravage.

walkthrough.title: Fast Power Phase
walkthrough.text: All players resolve their Fast powers now. You can resolve them in any order that helps the team. Check each card's range and targeting requirements — most powers specify a land type, a range from your Presence, and a required threshold of invaders or Dahan. Coordinate with your teammates to cover the most critical lands.

walkthrough.title: Invader Phase: Blighted Island Check
walkthrough.text: Now the invaders act. First, check if any earned Fear cards should resolve. If the Fear Pool has generated enough fear, flip Fear cards and apply their effects — they usually help the Spirits. Then the invader pipeline resolves in strict order: first Ravage, then Build, then Explore.

walkthrough.title: Invader Phase: Ravage
walkthrough.text: In every land matching that card's type, invaders deal damage. Each Explorer deals one damage, each Town deals two, and each City deals three. Add up the total. If the damage is two or more, add one Blight token to that land from the Blight card. Then the Dahan fight back — each Dahan deals two damage to invaders in that land.

walkthrough.title: Invader Phase: Build
walkthrough.text: Now look at the Build slot card. In every land matching that type where invaders are present, they build. If no Town or City exists in that land, add one Town. If a Town already exists, upgrade one Town to a City. The invaders are spreading and fortifying. This is where unchecked pressure turns into real problems.

walkthrough.title: Invader Phase: Explore
walkthrough.text: Finally, the Explore slot. Flip the top card of the Invader Deck and resolve it. Add one Explorer to every land of that type that is adjacent to a Town, City, or Ocean. Then slide the cards along the pipeline: the Ravage card is discarded, the Build card moves to Ravage, and the new Explore card moves to Build. Now you can see what is coming next round.

walkthrough.title: The Invader Pipeline
walkthrough.text: This pipeline is the heartbeat of the game. A land type is revealed in Explore this round, then invaders Build there next round, then Ravage the round after. You always have advance warning. Use this knowledge to plan your powers. If you see Jungle in the Build slot, you know Jungle lands will be Ravaged next round — get your defenses ready.

walkthrough.title: Blight and Cascades
walkthrough.text: Blight is the island's health meter. When Blight is added to a land that already has Blight, it cascades — one additional Blight spills into an adjacent land. If that adjacent land also has Blight, it cascades again. Cascades can chain across the island. When the Blight card runs out of tokens, flip it to the Blighted side and read the new rules. If blight overruns the island, you lose.

walkthrough.title: Fear and Terror Levels
walkthrough.text: You generate Fear by destroying invader pieces. Destroying a Town generates one Fear, and destroying a City generates two. When the Fear Pool is empty, you earn a Fear card which resolves during the next Invader Phase. As you work through the Fear deck, you hit Terror Level dividers. Higher Terror levels make the win condition easier.

walkthrough.title: Slow Power Phase
walkthrough.text: After the invaders have acted, all Slow powers resolve. This is your chance to deal damage, push invaders around, or set up defenses for next round. Slow powers tend to be stronger than Fast powers because they cannot prevent the current round's Ravage. Use them to clean up damage or preemptively weaken next round's threats.

walkthrough.title: Time Passes
walkthrough.text: The final phase of each round is Time Passes. Discard all played Power cards to your personal discard pile. Remove all damage markers and temporary effect tokens. Check for any end-of-round triggers. Then a new round begins with another Spirit Phase.

walkthrough.title: Invader Piece Health
walkthrough.text: Let's clarify how damage works on invader pieces. Explorers have one health, so one damage destroys them. Towns have two health, so two damage destroys a Town. Cities have three health. Dahan also have two health. Damage carries over within a single power resolution, so three damage to a land with one Explorer and one Town destroys both.

walkthrough.title: Defend Powers
walkthrough.text: Some powers Defend a land, reducing incoming Ravage damage by a specified amount. If you Defend three in a land where invaders would deal four damage, only one damage gets through — not enough to cause Blight. Defend is one of the most important tools for keeping the island alive, especially in early rounds before you can destroy invaders outright.

walkthrough.title: Push and Gather
walkthrough.text: Powers let you move pieces between lands. Push moves invaders or Dahan out of a land into adjacent lands. Gather pulls them in. These are powerful tactical tools. Push invaders out of a land about to be defended, or Gather Dahan into a land where they can fight back during Ravage. Movement is often better than raw damage.

walkthrough.title: Gaining New Powers
walkthrough.text: When your Growth option includes Gain a Power Card, you choose Minor or Major. For Minor, draw four cards, pick one, and discard the rest. For Major, draw four Major cards, pick one, and then you must forget (permanently discard) one of your existing Power cards. Major powers are expensive but devastating. Balance your power gains with your Energy economy.

walkthrough.title: Reclaiming Cards
walkthrough.text: When you Reclaim cards through Growth, you pick up all your discarded Power cards back into your hand. This is essential because played cards go to your discard pile at the end of each round. Without reclaiming, you will run out of cards to play. Plan your Reclaim timing carefully — some Spirits need to reclaim every other round.

walkthrough.title: Victory Conditions
walkthrough.text: You win based on the current Terror Level. At Terror Level one, you must destroy every single invader on the island — no Explorers, no Towns, no Cities. At Terror Level two, you only need to clear all Towns and Cities. At Terror Level three, you only need to clear all Cities. If you earn all Fear cards, you win immediately regardless of what is on the board.

walkthrough.title: Defeat Conditions
walkthrough.text: You lose if any of three things happen. First, if the island is overrun by Blight — the Blight card's defeat condition triggers. Second, if any Spirit is completely destroyed, meaning all its Presence is removed from the island. Third, if you need to draw an Invader card and the deck is empty. The game is cooperative, so if any of these happen, everyone loses together.

No individual turns — cooperative timing

walkthrough.title: Welcome, Island Defenders
walkthrough.text: In this practice tutorial, we will walk through a scripted first round with two Spirits defending the island together. Spirit A and Spirit B each have their Presence on the island, four Power cards in hand, and the invaders are already threatening. Let's save this island.

walkthrough.title: Read the Invader Pipeline
walkthrough.text: Before doing anything, let's read the Invader Board. From setup, there is already one Invader card in the Build slot — let's say it shows Wetland. That means Wetland lands will have invaders Build there this round, and then Ravage next round. Understanding this pipeline is the most important skill in the game.

walkthrough.title: Spirit Phase: Spirit A Chooses Growth
walkthrough.text: Adding Presence extends their reach and uncovers a better value on their Energy track. They place the Presence token on a Jungle land adjacent to existing Presence. Their Energy track now shows two instead of one.

walkthrough.title: Spirit Phase: Spirit B Chooses Growth
walkthrough.text: They draw four Minor Power cards, examine them, and keep the one with a useful Defend effect. The other three go to the Minor discard pile. They place their new Presence on a Mountain land.

walkthrough.title: Gain Energy and Play Cards
walkthrough.text: Both Spirits gain Energy from their top Presence track. Spirit A gains two Energy, Spirit B gains one. Now they choose Power cards. Spirit A plays two cards — one Fast and one Slow, paying their Energy costs. Spirit B plays one Fast card. Cards are placed face up so both players can see what is planned.

walkthrough.title: Planning Together
walkthrough.text: Let's talk about what the team sees. The Wetland Build will happen this round, adding Towns or Cities. There is also a Jungle land with an Explorer and a Town that was set up to Ravage this round. Spirit A's Fast power can Defend that Jungle land. Spirit B's Fast power can push the Explorer out of a threatening Wetland. Let's coordinate.

walkthrough.title: Fast Power Phase
walkthrough.text: Spirit A plays their Fast power on the Jungle land about to be Ravaged. It Defends three, which means the invaders' damage will be reduced by three. The Town deals two damage, and the Explorer deals one, for a total of three. With Defend three, zero damage gets through. No Blight this round — great play.

walkthrough.title: Spirit B's Mistake: Wrong Target
walkthrough.text: Spirit B plays their Fast power to push an Explorer out of a Sands land. The push works — the Explorer moves to an adjacent land. But wait. The Wetland Build is the real threat this round, and Spirit B's power had the range to reach a Wetland with two Explorers. Pushing one of those Explorers out would have prevented a Town from being built there.

walkthrough.title: Lesson: Prioritize the Pipeline
walkthrough.text: Always target your powers based on the invader pipeline. The biggest threats are lands about to Ravage and lands about to Build. Spirit B used their power on a Sands land that was not in immediate danger, while a Wetland Build was about to create a new Town. Read the pipeline, identify the highest-consequence lands, and target those first.

walkthrough.title: Invader Phase: Fear Cards
walkthrough.text: Let's check the Fear Pool. The team has not generated enough fear yet for a Fear card, so we skip straight to the invader actions. Fear generation will become more important in later rounds as you start destroying Towns and Cities.

walkthrough.title: Invader Phase: Ravage
walkthrough.text: The Ravage card shows Jungle. In each Jungle land with invaders, they deal damage. In one Jungle, Spirit A's Defend absorbed all damage. In another Jungle, a lone Explorer deals one damage — not enough for Blight since the threshold is two. The Dahan in that land strike back, dealing two damage and destroying the Explorer. Dahan are your allies — preserve them.

walkthrough.title: Invader Phase: Build
walkthrough.text: The Build card shows Wetland. In every Wetland with invaders, they build. One Wetland has two Explorers — a Town is added. Another has a Town — it upgrades to a City. If Spirit B had pushed one of those Explorers earlier, that Town would not have appeared. Mistakes compound in Spirit Island.

walkthrough.title: Invader Phase: Explore
walkthrough.text: A new Invader card flips — let's say it is Mountain. One Explorer is added to each Mountain land adjacent to a Town, City, or Ocean. The pipeline slides forward: the Jungle Ravage card is discarded, the Wetland card moves to Ravage, and Mountain goes to Build. Now you can see two rounds ahead. Wetland will Ravage next round.

walkthrough.title: Spirit B's Mistake: Skipping Growth
walkthrough.text: Let's rewind to a decision Spirit B almost made. Spirit B considered skipping their Growth to save time, just playing cards with their existing Energy and Presence. This would have been a serious error. Skipping Growth means no new Presence, no track advancement, and stagnant Energy gain. You would fall behind the invaders' accelerating pressure.

walkthrough.title: Lesson: Never Skip Growth
walkthrough.text: Growth is how your Spirit scales to match the invaders' escalating threat. Every round without Growth is a round where your Energy gain, card play limit, and island reach stay flat while the invaders keep expanding. Even if no Growth option seems perfect, pick the one that advances your economy. Growth now pays dividends every future round.

walkthrough.title: Slow Power Phase
walkthrough.text: Spirit A's Slow power resolves now. It deals two damage to invaders in a land. They target the Wetland that just had a Town built — two damage destroys the new Town and generates one Fear. That is how Slow powers work. You could not prevent the Build, but you can destroy what was built before it causes Ravage damage next round.

walkthrough.title: Spirit B's Mistake: Ignoring Fear
walkthrough.text: Spirit B had a choice between dealing one damage to an Explorer or dealing two damage to a Town. They chose to destroy the Explorer, thinking any removal helps. But destroying a Town generates one Fear, while destroying an Explorer generates zero. Fear pushes you toward easier Terror Level win conditions. Spirit B missed a strategic opportunity.

walkthrough.title: Lesson: Fear Matters
walkthrough.text: Fear generation is not a side effect — it is a core strategy. Every Fear you generate pushes the Terror Level closer to easier victory conditions. At Terror Level three, you only need to destroy Cities instead of every invader on the board. When choosing targets, prioritize Towns and Cities over Explorers when the damage math allows it.

walkthrough.title: Time Passes
walkthrough.text: The round is over. Both Spirits discard their played Power cards to their personal discard piles. Temporary effects are cleared. Now look at the pipeline — Wetland Ravage is coming next round, and Mountain Build after that. Plan your next Spirit Phase around these threats.

walkthrough.title: Round Two Preview
walkthrough.text: Going into Round two, the team has learned important lessons. Read the pipeline before making any decisions. Target the highest-consequence lands first. Never skip Growth. Generate Fear whenever possible. Spirit Island rewards planning and punishes reactive play.

walkthrough.title: Congratulations
walkthrough.text: Well done, defenders! You have survived your first full round of Spirit Island. You have seen how the Spirit Phase, Fast powers, invader pipeline, and Slow powers all interact. You watched three mistakes and learned why pipeline reading, Growth investment, and Fear generation are critical to victory.

walkthrough.title: Continue Your Defense
walkthrough.text: Before your next game, check out General Tips for advice on Spirit coordination and common traps to avoid. The Appendix has a complete reference for all game terms and invader rules. And remember — your personal GM Guide is here to answer any question during play. Just tap the Q&A tab. The island is counting on you!

walkthrough.title: Read the Pipeline First
walkthrough.text: At the start of every Spirit Phase, before you choose Growth or play cards, look at the Invader Board. Identify what is about to Ravage, what will Build, and what just Explored. This tells you exactly where the danger is. Every decision you make this round should flow from that pipeline reading. Players who react after the fact lose. Players who read ahead survive.

walkthrough.title: Triage, Do Not Try to Solve Everything
walkthrough.text: You cannot stop every invader action, especially in early rounds. Accept this and triage. Ask three questions about each threatened land: Will Ravage here cause Blight? Will that Blight cascade? Will losing this land cost us the game? Focus your limited powers on the highest-consequence lands. Let low-impact Ravages happen. A single Blight in an empty corner is fine.

walkthrough.title: Grow Your Economy Early
walkthrough.text: It is tempting to spend every round just fighting invaders, but you must invest in Growth. Adding Presence to the island uncovers better Energy gain and higher card play limits. If you stall your economy to handle turn-to-turn fires, the invaders will outscale you by round four or five. Commit to at least one meaningful Growth action every round.

walkthrough.title: Coordinate Land Ownership
walkthrough.text: In multiplayer games, the most common mistake is two Spirits spending powers on the same land while another land burns unchecked. At the start of each Spirit Phase, quickly assign land ownership. Spirit A handles the Jungle Ravage, Spirit B handles the Wetland Build. This takes thirty seconds of planning and prevents wasted powers.

walkthrough.title: Preserve Your Dahan
walkthrough.text: Dahan are not just flavor. Each Dahan deals two damage to invaders during Ravage, which is as much as a Town deals. A land with three Dahan can destroy a Town and an Explorer without you spending any Power cards. Defend lands with Dahan clusters and Gather Dahan toward upcoming Ravage zones. They are free damage every round.

walkthrough.title: Match Card Speed to Threat Timing
walkthrough.text: A powerful Slow card cannot prevent this round's Ravage — it resolves afterward. If a land desperately needs protection right now, only a Fast power or a Defend can help. Conversely, Slow powers are perfect for destroying invaders that just Built, before they Ravage next round. Always check whether the threat is this round or next round before choosing which card to play.

walkthrough.title: Track the Blight Clock
walkthrough.text: Blight is your loss clock, not just local damage. Count how many Blight tokens remain on the Blight card every round. If you are down to two or three, you need to stop all Ravages that could cause Blight. If the Blight card flips to the Blighted side, read the new rules carefully — some add harsh penalties. Preventing Blight is more important than destroying invaders when the clock is low.

walkthrough.title: Fear Is a Win Condition
walkthrough.text: Generating Fear is not a bonus — it is a primary path to victory. Every Terror Level makes the win condition dramatically easier. At Terror three, you only need to clear Cities. Some games are won entirely through Fear pressure without ever clearing the board. Include Fear generation in your plan from round one. Prioritize destroying Towns and Cities over Explorers whenever the damage math allows.

summary.title: Read the Pipeline First
bullets: Check Ravage, Build, Explore slots before any decisions
All round planning flows from pipeline reading
Proactive play > reactive play

Focus on: Blight risk, cascade risk, game-losing potential
Let low-impact Ravages happen

At least 1 meaningful Growth action per round

Prevents double-covering one land while another burns
30 seconds of planning saves wasted powers

bullets: Each Dahan = 2 free damage during Ravage
Defend Dahan clusters, Gather them toward threats
3 Dahan can destroy a Town + Explorer with no Power cards

summary.title: Match Card Speed to Threat Timing
bullets: Fast/Defend = this round's Ravage protection
Slow = clean up after Ravage or preempt next round
Wrong speed = wasted power

summary.title: Track the Blight Clock
bullets: Count Blight tokens on card every round
Low Blight = stop all Ravage-causing Blight at all costs
Blight card flip → harsh new rules

summary.title: Fear Is a Win Condition
bullets: Each Terror Level = dramatically easier win condition
Terror III: only Cities must be destroyed
Prioritize Towns/Cities over Explorers for Fear generation

walkthrough.title: The Prevent Line vs The Recover Line
walkthrough.text: Every round presents a core strategic choice. The Prevent line uses Fast powers and Defend to stop Ravage damage before it happens. The Recover line lets damage occur and uses Slow powers to destroy the invaders afterward. Prevention is generally safer but requires Fast cards and good timing. Recovery lets you use stronger Slow powers but risks Blight. The best teams mix both approaches based on what each Spirit is built to do.

walkthrough.title: Presence Placement Strategy
walkthrough.text: Where you place Presence is one of the most impactful decisions in the game. Local placement solves immediate range problems — you can target nearby lands with your powers. Network placement extends your reach across multiple boards for future flexibility. Early game, prioritize local placement to handle your assigned zone. Mid game, start extending toward adjacent boards so you can assist teammates when critical threats emerge.

walkthrough.title: Energy Economy Management
walkthrough.text: Energy management separates good players from great ones. Playing your strongest cards every round feels powerful but will bankrupt you. Instead, alternate between high-spend rounds where you play multiple expensive powers, and low-spend rounds where you play one cheap card and bank Energy for next round. This rhythm keeps you flexible and ready for emergencies.

walkthrough.title: Minor vs Major Power Decisions
walkthrough.text: Minor Powers are cheap, flexible, and easy to fit into any round. Major Powers are expensive game-changers that can clear entire lands or generate massive Fear. The key question is: can your Energy economy support a Major? If you are gaining three or more Energy per round and have a healthy track, pick up a Major. If your economy is still developing, stick with Minors until you can afford the big plays.

walkthrough.title: Fear Race Strategy
walkthrough.text: Some Spirit combinations are built to win through Fear rather than board control. The Fear race focuses on destroying as many Towns and Cities as possible each round, even at the cost of taking Blight elsewhere. Each Town destroyed generates one Fear, each City generates two. Push aggressively through Terror Levels until the win condition becomes achievable. This strategy works best with high-damage Spirits who can reliably destroy multiple pieces per round.

walkthrough.title: Board Control Strategy
walkthrough.text: The opposite of the Fear race is board control — methodically clearing sections of the island so invader actions fizzle. If there are no invaders in a land, Build and Ravage do nothing there. Push Explorers away before they Build. Destroy Towns before they upgrade to Cities. Control players focus on preventing the invader snowball rather than generating Fear. This strategy works best with Spirits who excel at movement and area denial.

walkthrough.title: Dahan as a Weapon
walkthrough.text: Advanced players actively position Dahan as offensive weapons, not just collateral survivors. Each Dahan counterattacks for two damage during Ravage. A land with four Dahan deals eight damage, enough to destroy a City and a Town. Gather Dahan into lands that are about to Ravage, then Defend the land so the Dahan survive to counterattack. Some powers also let Dahan attack outside of the Ravage phase.

walkthrough.title: Cascade Prevention
walkthrough.text: Blight cascades are the most dangerous mechanic in Spirit Island. One cascade can chain across the board, potentially flipping the Blight card and triggering defeat. Prevent cascades by tracking which lands already have Blight and defending those aggressively. If Blight is inevitable, Push or Gather to redirect it to a land without existing Blight. Cascade awareness should inform every defensive decision you make.

walkthrough.title: Spirit Synergies
walkthrough.text: The Spirits in Spirit Island are designed with asymmetric strengths that complement each other. A defensive Spirit paired with an offensive Spirit covers both survival and elimination. A Fear-generating Spirit paired with a control Spirit pushes Terror while maintaining board stability. Before the game starts, discuss your Spirits' strengths and assign roles: who defends, who attacks, who generates Fear, and who controls movement.

walkthrough.title: When to Add Difficulty
walkthrough.text: Once your team wins consistently at base difficulty, add an Adversary at Level one. Adversaries change the invader rules, adding new Build and Explore patterns. Increase Adversary level only when you win more often than you lose. Scenarios add thematic challenges with unique victory conditions. Do not stack an Adversary and a Scenario until you are comfortable with each individually. Difficulty progression keeps the game fresh for hundreds of plays.

summary.title: The Prevent Line vs The Recover Line
bullets: Prevent: Fast powers + Defend → stop Ravage damage
Recover: let damage happen → Slow powers destroy invaders after
Best teams mix both based on Spirit strengths

summary.title: Presence Placement Strategy
bullets: Local placement: solve immediate range needs
Network placement: extend reach for future flexibility
Early = local, mid-game = extend toward adjacent boards

summary.title: Energy Economy Management
bullets: Alternate high-spend and low-spend rounds
Bank Energy for emergencies and Major Power plays
Playing max cards every round → bankrupt by mid-game

Take Majors when gaining 3+ Energy/round

summary.title: Fear Race Strategy

Best with high-damage Spirits

Don't stack Adversary + Scenario until comfortable with each

entries.definition: A unique playable entity with its own Growth options, Presence tracks, innate powers, and four starting unique Power cards. Each Spirit plays differently and excels at different roles.
The double-sided board for each Spirit. The front shows Growth options, Presence tracks, innate powers, and special rules. The back shows starting Presence placement on the island.
Tokens placed on the island representing a Spirit's influence. Presence determines range for targeting powers. Removing Presence from tracks uncovers better Energy and card play values.
The top track on the Spirit panel. Shows Energy gained per round. As Presence is moved to the island, higher values are uncovered.
The bottom track on the Spirit panel. Shows maximum Power cards playable per round. Uncovering higher values lets you play more cards.
Currency used to pay Power card costs. Gained each round from the Presence track and some Growth options. Unspent Energy carries over between rounds.
The first step of the Spirit Phase. Choose one Growth option from your Spirit panel to add Presence, gain Energy, Reclaim cards, or gain new Power cards.
The first phase of each round. All players simultaneously choose Growth, gain Energy, and select Power cards to play.
Resolves all Fast-speed powers before the Invader Phase. Used for Defend, prevention, and emergency actions.
The phase where invaders act. Resolves in order: Fear cards, Ravage, Build, Explore. Driven by the Invader Board pipeline.
Resolves all Slow-speed powers after the Invader Phase. Slow powers tend to be stronger since they cannot prevent current-round Ravage.
Final phase of each round. Discard played Power cards, clear temporary effects, and check for end-of-round triggers.
A Power card with a bird icon. Resolves before the Invader Phase, allowing prevention of Ravage damage through Defend, Push, or other effects.
A Power card with a turtle icon. Resolves after the Invader Phase. Typically stronger effects than Fast powers but cannot prevent current-round damage.
Lower-cost Power cards from the Minor deck. Draw four, keep one when gaining. Flexible and affordable for any Spirit.
High-cost, high-impact Power cards from the Major deck. Draw four, keep one, but must forget one existing Power card. Game-changing but expensive.
The four starting Power cards specific to each Spirit. They define the Spirit's core abilities and playstyle.
A power printed on the Spirit panel that activates automatically when you have played enough cards with matching elements. No Energy cost required.
A Growth action that returns all discarded Power cards to your hand. Essential for maintaining card supply since played cards are discarded each round.
Permanently remove a Power card from your hand or discard. Required when gaining a Major Power. The forgotten card is removed from the game.
A power effect that reduces incoming Ravage damage in a target land by a specified amount. Defend three means three less damage from invaders.
A power effect that moves one or more pieces (invaders or Dahan) from a target land to an adjacent land. Used to reposition threats or allies.
A power effect that moves one or more pieces from adjacent lands into a target land. Useful for concentrating Dahan for counterattacks.
The weakest invader piece. One health, deals one damage during Ravage. Added to the island during Explore. Destroying an Explorer generates zero Fear.
A mid-tier invader piece. Two health, deals two damage during Ravage. Built when an Explorer-only land has a Build action. Destroying a Town generates one Fear.
The strongest invader piece. Three health, deals three damage during Ravage. Created when a Town is upgraded during Build. Destroying a City generates two Fear.
Native islanders who fight alongside the Spirits. Two health, deal two counterattack damage during Ravage. Preserving and positioning Dahan is a key strategy.
Tokens representing environmental destruction. Added to a land when invaders deal two or more Ravage damage. Causes cascades if added to a land that already has Blight.
When Blight is added to a land that already has Blight, one additional Blight must be added to an adjacent land. If that land also has Blight, it cascades again. Chains can devastate the island.

A card placed during setup with Blight tokens on it. When all tokens are removed, flip to the Blighted side for new rules. Some Blighted-side effects trigger immediate penalties or altered loss conditions.
Modular board pieces representing sections of the island. Each has printed terrain types and starting piece icons. One board per player.
The five terrain types on the island: Jungle, Wetland, Sands, Mountain, and Ocean. Invader cards target specific land types. Ocean is not a playable land but counts for adjacency.
The central board tracking the invader pipeline with slots for Ravage, Build, and Explore cards. Also holds the Fear Pool and Invader Deck.
A 12-card deck built from Stages I, II, and III. One card flipped per round for Explore. Stage progression increases invader pressure over time.
The three-slot system on the Invader Board. A card enters at Explore, slides to Build next round, then Ravage the round after. Provides advance warning of threats.
The first invader action each round. Invaders in matching lands deal damage: Explorer one, Town two, City three. Two or more total damage adds Blight. Dahan counterattack afterward.
The second invader action. In matching lands with invaders: add a Town if none exists, or upgrade a Town to a City. Unchecked Builds cause exponential growth.
The third invader action. Add one Explorer to each matching land adjacent to a Town, City, or Ocean. New Invader card flipped from deck.
Generated by destroying invader pieces. Town destruction yields one Fear, City destruction yields two. When the Fear Pool empties, a Fear card is earned.
Earned when the Fear Pool empties. Resolved at the start of the Invader Phase. Effects typically benefit the Spirits, such as removing invaders or adding Defend.
A collection of Fear markers on the Invader Board. Four markers per player. As Fear is generated, markers are moved out. Pool empty means a Fear card is earned.

Progresses as Fear cards are earned past Terror dividers. Level I requires all invaders destroyed to win. Level II only Towns and Cities. Level III only Cities. All Fear cards earned equals instant win.
An optional difficulty module representing a specific colonizing nation. Each Adversary changes invader rules at increasing difficulty levels. Add after mastering base game.
An optional thematic module with unique setup rules and victory conditions. Adds variety without necessarily increasing difficulty. Do not stack with Adversary until comfortable with each.
A first-game aid that tells you which Power cards to gain each round instead of drafting from the power decks. Simplifies early learning.
Symbols on Power cards representing thematic elements like Sun, Moon, Fire, Water, Earth, Plant, Animal, and Air. Playing cards with matching elements can activate innate powers on your Spirit panel.
The maximum distance from your Presence that a power can target. Range zero means the land must contain your Presence. Range one means adjacent to Presence. Checked when resolving the power.
A land containing two or more of your Presence tokens. Some powers require targeting from or at a Sacred Site for their effects to apply.
Two lands are adjacent if they share a printed border on the Island Board. Ocean is adjacent to coastal lands. Adjacency matters for Explore, Push, Gather, Cascade, and Range.
A land that borders the Ocean. Some Spirits and powers specifically target or benefit coastal lands.
